entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The primary crop species in which EST-SSR markers were developed and mapped.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop species whose genome was used for homology and synteny to design markers for sorghum.

entities.name: EST-SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Expressed Sequence Tag-Simple Sequence Repeat markers developed for sorghum.

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple sequence repeat markers used in molecular breeding programs.

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphisms mentioned as a marker system.

entities.name: EST
entities.description: Expressed sequence tags used as a source for marker development.

entities.name: SHOOTFLY RESISTANCE
entities.description: The specific trait being mapped in the sorghum population.

entities.name: SHOOTFLY
entities.description: The pest associated with the resistance trait being studied.

entities.name: PCR
entities.description: Polymerase chain reaction used to optimize primer pairs for markers.

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATIONS
entities.description: Groups of genotypes used to determine the location of markers on a chromosome.

entities.name: RIL MAPPING POPULATION
entities.description: Recombinant Inbred Line population used for screening polymorphic markers.

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of an organism.

entities.name: LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A map showing the relative positions of genes or markers on a chromosome.

entities.name: SKELETON MAP
entities.description: A basic existing map used as a framework for adding new markers.

entities.name: RICE-SORGHUM SYNTENY
entities.description: The conserved order of genes between the rice and sorghum genomes.

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: The use of molecular markers to improve crop traits.

entities.name: J. CRAIG RESEARCH INSTITUTE
entities.description: The institution providing the database used for mining EST sequences.
